data.title: Economist cautioning Trump's $200B housing stimulus will raise prices, not restore affordability due to increased demand.
data.description: Moody's Chief Economist, Mark Zandi, warns that President Trump's directive for Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac to purchase $200 billion in mortgage bonds may backfire by driving home prices higher instead of solving the nation's housing affordability problem. Despite Trump's claims that this move would restore affordability and the American Dream, Zandi argues that it ignores basic economics. Following the announcement, fixed mortgage rates decreased by 10-20 basis points to just over 6%, but Zandi cautions that this relief is illusory. While lower rates may support housing demand, the severe housing shortage will likely result in higher house prices. Zandi suggests that the influx of cash will bid up the cost of limited inventory, negating the benefits of slightly lower rates, making homebuying less affordable.

data.title: TomTom launches Area Analytics for precise traffic insights; tool lets users define and analyze traffic with hourly data.
data.description: On January 13, 2026, in Amsterdam, TomTom (TOM2), a leading mapping and location technology company, introduced Area Analytics. This new traffic analytics tool allows cities, governments, and organizations to analyze traffic within custom-defined areas over various periods. The tool provides detailed hourly data that can be examined for days, months, or years, offering a comprehensive understanding of mobility trends.

data.title: TomTom and Miovision join forces to enhance traffic optimization worldwide, leveraging analytics for better road networks.
data.description: On January 13, 2026, TomTom (TOM2) and Miovision unveiled a strategic partnership aimed at enhancing traffic management and infrastructure investments on a global scale. This collaboration integrates TomTom's Traffic Analytics suite with Miovision's innovative traffic software and hardware solutions. By merging their expertise, the two companies seek to enhance the safety and efficiency of road networks worldwide.

data.title: SEC chair unsure if US will confiscate Venezuela's reported $60B Bitcoin holdings; analysts unable to verify.
data.description: A number of experts in blockchain technology have reported that they were not able to confirm if the country in Latin America possesses $60 billion in cryptocurrency.
